translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Seeing that many did take in hand to set in order a narration of the matters that have been fully assured among us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as they did deliver to us, who from the beginning became eye-witnesses, and officers of the Word, --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: it seemed good also to me, having followed from the first after all things exactly, to write to thee in order, most noble Theophilus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that thou mayest know the certainty of the things wherein thou wast instructed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest, by name Zacharias, of the course of Abijah, and his wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name Elisabeth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were both righteous before God, going on in all the commands and righteousnesses of the Lord blameless,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they had no child, because that Elisabeth was barren, and both were advanced in their days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his acting as priest, in the order of his course before God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: according to the custom of the priesthood, his lot was to make perfume, having gone into the sanctuary of the Lord,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the multitude of the people were praying without, at the hour of the perfume.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there appeared to him a messenger of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of the perfume,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Zacharias, having seen, was troubled, and fear fell on him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the messenger said unto him, 'Fear not, Zacharias, for thy supplication was heard, and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear a son to thee, and thou shalt call his name John,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there shall be joy to thee, and gladness, and many at his birth shall joy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he shall be great before the Lord, and wine and strong drink he may not drink, and of the Holy Spirit he shall be full, even from his mother's womb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and many of the sons of Israel he shall turn to the Lord their God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall go before Him, in the spirit and power of Elijah, to turn hearts of fathers unto children, and disobedient ones to the wisdom of righteous ones, to make ready for the Lord, a people prepared.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zacharias said unto the messenger, 'Whereby shall I know this? for I am aged, and my wife is advanced in her days?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger answering said to him, 'I am Gabriel, who have been standing near before God, and I was sent to speak unto thee, and to proclaim these good news to thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, thou shalt be silent, and not able to speak, till the day that these things shall come to pass, because thou didst not believe my words, that shall be fulfilled in their season.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people were waiting for Zacharias, and wondering at his tarrying in the sanctuary,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come out, he was not able to speak to them, and they perceived that a vision he had seen in the sanctuary, and he was beckoning to them, and did remain dumb.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the days of his service were fulfilled, he went away to his house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and after those days, his wife Elisabeth conceived, and hid herself five months, saying --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thus hath the Lord done to me, in days in which He looked upon [me], to take away my reproach among men.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the sixth month was the messenger Gabriel sent by God, to a city of Galilee, the name of which [is] Nazareth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to a virgin, betrothed to a man, whose name [is] Joseph, of the house of David, and the name of the virgin [is] Mary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger having come in unto her, said, 'Hail, favoured one, the Lord [is] with thee; blessed [art] thou among women;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she, having seen, was troubled at his word, and was reasoning of what kind this salutation may be.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger said to her, 'Fear not, Mary, for thou hast found favour with God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, thou shalt conceive in the womb, and shalt bring forth a son, and call his name Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he shall be great, and Son of the Highest he shall be called, and the Lord God shall give him the throne of David his father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall reign over the house of Jacob to the ages; and of his reign there shall be no end.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mary said unto the messenger, 'How shall this be, seeing a husband I do not know?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger answering said to her, 'The Holy Spirit shall come upon thee, and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee, therefore also the holy-begotten thing shall be called Son of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, Elisabeth, thy kinswoman, she also hath conceived a son in her old age, and this is the sixth month to her who was called barren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because nothing shall be impossible with God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mary said, 'Lo, the maid-servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to thy saying,' and the messenger went away from her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mary having arisen in those days, went to the hill-country, with haste, to a city of Judea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and entered into the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elisabeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe did leap in her womb; and Elisabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and spake out with a loud voice, and said, 'Blessed [art] thou among women, and blessed [is] the fruit of thy womb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whence [is] this to me, that the mother of my Lord might come unto me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, lo, when the voice of thy salutation came to my ears, leap in gladness did the babe in my womb;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and happy [is] she who did believe, for there shall be a completion to the things spoken to her from the Lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mary said, 'My soul doth magnify the Lord,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my spirit was glad on God my Saviour,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because He looked on the lowliness of His maid-servant, For, lo, henceforth call me happy shall all the generations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He who is mighty did to me great things, And holy [is] His name,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And His kindness [is] to generations of generations, To those fearing Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He did powerfully with His arm, He scattered abroad the proud in the thought of their heart,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He brought down the mighty from thrones, And He exalted the lowly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The hungry He did fill with good, And the rich He sent away empty,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He received again Israel His servant, To remember kindness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As He spake unto our fathers, To Abraham and to his seed -- to the age.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Mary remained with her about three months, and turned back to her house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to Elisabeth was the time fulfilled for her bringing forth, and she bare a son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the neighbours and her kindred heard that the Lord was making His kindness great with her, and they were rejoicing with her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child, and they were calling him by the name of his father, Zacharias,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his mother answering said, 'No, but he shall be called John.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said unto her -- 'There is none among thy kindred who is called by this name,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were making signs to his father, what he would wish him to be called,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having asked for a tablet, he wrote, saying, 'John is his name;' and they did all wonder;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his mouth was opened presently, and his tongue, and he was speaking, praising God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And fear came upon all those dwelling around them, and in all the hill-country of Judea were all these sayings spoken of,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all who heard did lay them up in their hearts, saying, 'What then shall this child be?' and the hand of the Lord was with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zacharias his father was filled with the Holy Spirit, and did prophesy, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blessed [is] the Lord, the God of Israel, Because He did look upon, And wrought redemption for His people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And did raise an horn of salvation to us, In the house of David His servant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As He spake by the mouth of His holy prophets, Which have been from the age;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salvation from our enemies, And out of the hand of all hating us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To do kindness with our fathers, And to be mindful of His holy covenant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An oath that He sware to Abraham our father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To give to us, without fear, Out of the hand of our enemies having been delivered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To serve Him, in holiness and righteousness Before Him, all the days of our life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou, child, Prophet of the Highest Shalt thou be called; For thou shalt go before the face of the Lord, To prepare His ways.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To give knowledge of salvation to His people In remission of their sins,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Through the tender mercies of our God, In which the rising from on high did look upon us,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To give light to those sitting in darkness and death-shade, To guide our feet to a way of peace.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the child grew, and was strengthened in spirit, and he was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days, there went forth a decree from Caesar Augustus, that all the world be enrolled --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this enrolment first came to pass when Cyrenius was governor of Syria --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all were going to be enrolled, each to his proper city,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, to Judea, to the city of David, that is called Bethlehem, because of his being of the house and family of David,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to enrol himself with Mary his betrothed wife, being with child.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in their being there, the days were fulfilled for her bringing forth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she brought forth her son -- the first-born, and wrapped him up, and laid him down in the manger, because there was not for them a place in the guest-chamber.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were shepherds in the same region, lodging in the field, and keeping the night-watches over their flock,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a messenger of the Lord stood over them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they feared a great fear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger said to them, 'Fear not, for lo, I bring you good news of great joy, that shall be to all the people --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because there was born to you to-day a Saviour -- who is Christ the Lord -- in the city of David,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this [is] to you the sign: Ye shall find a babe wrapped up, lying in the manger.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And suddenly there came with the messenger a multitude of the heavenly host, praising God, and saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Glory in the highest to God, and upon earth peace, among men -- good will.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when the messengers were gone away from them to the heavens, that the men, the shepherds, said unto one another, 'We may go over indeed unto Bethlehem, and see this thing that hath come to pass, that the Lord did make known to us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they came, having hasted, and found both Mary, and Joseph, and the babe lying in the manger,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen, they made known abroad concerning the saying spoken to them concerning the child.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all who heard, did wonder concerning the things spoken by the shepherds unto them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Mary was preserving all these things, pondering in her heart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the shepherds turned back, glorifying and praising God, for all those things they heard and saw, as it was spoken unto them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when eight days were fulfilled to circumcise the child, then was his name called Jesus, having been so called by the messenger before his being conceived in the womb.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the days of their purification were fulfilled, according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present to the Lord,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as it hath been written in the Law of the Lord, -- 'Every male opening a womb shall be called holy to the Lord,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to give a sacrifice, according to that said in the Law of the Lord, 'A pair of turtle-doves, or two young pigeons.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name [is] Simeon, and this man is righteous and devout, looking for the comforting of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it hath been divinely told him by the Holy Spirit -- not to see death before he may see the Christ of the Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he came in the Spirit to the temple, and in the parents bringing in the child Jesus, for their doing according to the custom of the law regarding him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then he took him in his arms, and blessed God, and he said,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Now Thou dost send away Thy servant, Lord, according to Thy word, in peace,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because mine eyes did see Thy salvation,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which Thou didst prepare before the face of all the peoples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a light to the uncovering of nations, and the glory of Thy people Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph and his mother were wondering at the things spoken concerning him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Simeon blessed them, and said unto Mary his mother, 'Lo, this [one] is set for the falling and rising again of many in Israel, and for a sign spoken against --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (and also thine own soul shall a sword pass through) -- that the reasonings of many hearts may be revealed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there was Anna, a prophetess, daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher, she was much advanced in days, having lived with an husband seven years from her virginity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she [is] a widow of about eighty-four years, who did depart not from the temple, with fasts and supplications serving, night and day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she, at that hour, having come in, was confessing, likewise, to the Lord, and was speaking concerning him, to all those looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when they finished all things, according to the Law of the Lord, they turned back to Galilee, to their city Nazareth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the child grew and was strengthened in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his parents were going yearly to Jerusalem, at the feast of the passover,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when he became twelve years old, they having gone up to Jerusalem, according to the custom of the feast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having finished the days, in their returning the child Jesus remained behind in Jerusalem, and Joseph and his mother did not know,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, having supposed him to be in the company, they went a day's journey, and were seeking him among the kindred and among the acquaintances,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not having found him, they turned back to Jerusalem seeking him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, after three days, they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both hearing them and questioning them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all those hearing him were astonished at his understanding and answers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, having seen him, they were amazed, and his mother said unto him, 'Child, why didst thou thus to us? lo, thy father and I, sorrowing, were seeking thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Why [is it] that ye were seeking me? did ye not know that in the things of my Father it behoveth me to be?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did not understand the saying that he spake to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and he was subject to them, and his mother was keeping all these sayings in her heart,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus was advancing in wisdom, and in stature, and in favour with God and men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the fifteenth year of the government of Tiberius Caesar -- Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, and Herod tetrarch of Galilee, and Philip his brother, tetrarch of Ituraea and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Annas and Caiaphas being chief priests -- there came a word of God unto John the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he came to all the region round the Jordan, proclaiming a baptism of reformation -- to remission of sins,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: as it hath been written in the scroll of the words of Isaiah the prophet, saying, 'A voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight make ye His paths;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall become straightness, and the rough become smooth ways;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all flesh shall see the salvation of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said he to the multitudes coming forth to be baptised by him, 'Brood of vipers! who did prompt you to flee from the coming wrath?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: make, therefore, fruits worthy of the reformation, and begin not to say within yourselves, We have a father -- Abraham; for I say to you, that God is able out of these stones to raise children to Abraham;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and already also the axe unto the root of the trees is laid, every tree, therefore, not making good fruit is cut down, and to fire it is cast.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitudes were questioning him, saying, 'What, then, shall we do?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering saith to them, 'He having two coats -- let him impart to him having none, and he having victuals -- in like manner let him do.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came also tax-gatherers to be baptised, and they said unto him, 'Teacher, what shall we do?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto them, 'Exact no more than that directed you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And questioning him also were those warring, saying, 'And we, what shall we do?' and he said unto them, 'Do violence to no one, nor accuse falsely, and be content with your wages.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people are looking forward, and all are reasoning in their hearts concerning John, whether or not he may be the Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: John answered, saying to all, 'I indeed with water do baptise you, but he cometh who is mightier than I, of whom I am not worthy to loose the latchet of his sandals -- he shall baptise you with the Holy Spirit and with fire;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whose winnowing shovel [is] in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his floor, and will gather the wheat to his storehouse, and the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, therefore, indeed with many other things, exhorting, he was proclaiming good news to the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him concerning Herodias the wife of Philip his brother, and concerning all the evils that Herod did,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: added also this to all, that he shut up John in the prison.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in all the people being baptised, Jesus also being baptised, and praying, the heaven was opened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Holy Spirit came down in a bodily appearance, as if a dove, upon him, and a voice came out of heaven, saying, 'Thou art My Son -- the Beloved, in thee I did delight.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus himself was beginning to be about thirty years of age, being, as was supposed, son of Joseph,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Eli, the [son] of Matthat, the [son] of Levi, the [son] of Melchi, the [son] of Janna, the [son] of Joseph,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Mattathias, the [son] of Amos, the [son] of Naum, the [son] of Esli,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Naggai, the [son] of Maath, the [son] of Mattathias, the [son] of Semei, the [son] of Joseph, the [son] of Juda,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Joanna, the [son] of Rhesa, the [son] of Zerubbabel, the [son] of Shealtiel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Neri, the [son] of Melchi, the [son] of Addi, the [son] of Cosam, the [son] of Elmodam, the [son] of Er,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Jose, the [son] of Eliezer, the [son] of Jorim, the [son] of Matthat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Levi, the [son] of Simeon, the [son] of Juda, the [son] of Joseph, the [son] of Jonan, the [son] of Eliakim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Melea, the [son] of Mainan, the [son] of Mattatha, the [son] of Nathan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of David, the [son] of Jesse, the [son] of Obed, the [son] of Booz, the [son] of Salmon, the [son] of Nahshon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Amminadab, the [son] of Aram, the [son] of Esrom, the [son] of Pharez,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Judah, the [son] of Jacob, the [son] of Isaac, the [son] of Abraham, the [son] of Terah, the [son] of Nahor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Serug, the [son] of Reu, the [son] of Peleg, the [son] of Eber,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Salah, the [son] of Cainan, the [son] of Arphaxad, the [son] of Shem, the [son] of Noah, the [son] of Lamech,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Methuselah, the [son] of Enoch, the [son] of Jared, the [son] of Mahalaleel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the [son] of Cainan, the [son] of Enos, the [son] of Seth, the [son] of Adam, the [son] of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, turned back from the Jordan, and was brought in the Spirit to the wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: forty days being tempted by the Devil, and he did not eat anything in those days, and they having been ended, he afterward hungered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Devil said to him, 'If Son thou art of God, speak to this stone that it may become bread.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answered him, saying, 'It hath been written, that, not on bread only shall man live, but on every saying of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Devil having brought him up to an high mountain, shewed to him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Devil said to him, 'To thee I will give all this authority, and their glory, because to me it hath been delivered, and to whomsoever I will, I do give it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thou, then, if thou mayest bow before me -- all shall be thine.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering him said, 'Get thee behind me, Adversary, for it hath been written, Thou shalt bow before the Lord thy God, and Him only thou shalt serve.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, 'If the Son thou art of God, cast thyself down hence,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it hath been written -- To His messengers He will give charge concerning thee, to guard over thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and -- On hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou mayest dash against a stone thy foot.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him -- 'It hath been said, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having ended all temptation, the Devil departed from him till a convenient season.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus turned back in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and a fame went forth through all the region round about concerning him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was teaching in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he came to Nazareth, where he hath been brought up, and he went in, according to his custom, on the sabbath-day, to the synagogue, and stood up to read;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there was given over to him a roll of Isaiah the prophet, and having unfolded the roll, he found the place where it hath been written:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The Spirit of the Lord [is] upon me, Because He did anoint me; To proclaim good news to the poor, Sent me to heal the broken of heart, To proclaim to captives deliverance, And to blind receiving of sight, To send away the bruised with deliverance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having folded the roll, having given [it] back to the officer, he sat down, and the eyes of all in the synagogue were gazing on him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he began to say unto them -- 'To-day hath this writing been fulfilled in your ears;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all were bearing testimony to him, and were wondering at the gracious words that are coming forth out of his mouth, and they said, 'Is not this the son of Joseph?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Certainly ye will say to me this simile, Physician, heal thyself; as great things as we heard done in Capernaum, do also here in thy country;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'Verily I say to you -- No prophet is accepted in his own country;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of a truth I say to you, Many widows were in the days of Elijah, in Israel, when the heaven was shut for three years and six months, when great famine came on all the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and unto none of them was Elijah sent, but -- to Sarepta of Sidon, unto a woman, a widow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and many lepers were in the time of Elisha the prophet, in Israel, and none of them was cleansed, but -- Naaman the Syrian.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all in the synagogue were filled with wrath, hearing these things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having risen, they put him forth without the city, and brought him unto the brow of the hill on which their city had been built -- to cast him down headlong,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he, having gone through the midst of them, went away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and was teaching them on the sabbaths,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were astonished at his teaching, because his word was with authority.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the synagogue was a man, having a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a great voice,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Away, what -- to us and to thee, Jesus, O Nazarene? thou didst come to destroy us; I have known thee who thou art -- the Holy One of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus did rebuke him, saying, 'Be silenced, and come forth out of him;' and the demon having cast him into the midst, came forth from him, having hurt him nought;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and amazement came upon all, and they were speaking together, with one another, saying, 'What [is] this word, that with authority and power he doth command the unclean spirits, and they come forth?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there was going forth a fame concerning him to every place of the region round about.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having risen out of the synagogue, he entered into the house of Simon, and the mother-in-law of Simon was pressed with a great fever, and they did ask him about her,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having stood over her, he rebuked the fever, and it left her, and presently, having risen, she was ministering to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And at the setting of the sun, all, as many as had any ailing with manifold sicknesses, brought them unto him, and he on each one of them [his] hands having put, did heal them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And demons also were coming forth from many, crying out and saying -- 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of God;' and rebuking, he did not suffer them to speak, because they knew him to be the Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And day having come, having gone forth, he went on to a desert place, and the multitudes were seeking him, and they came unto him, and were staying him -- not to go on from them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto them -- 'Also to the other cities it behoveth me to proclaim good news of the reign of God, because for this I have been sent;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in the multitude pressing on him to hear the word of God, that he was standing beside the lake of Gennesaret,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saw two boats standing beside the lake, and the fishers, having gone away from them, were washing the nets,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having entered into one of the boats, that was Simon's, he asked him to put back a little from the land, and having sat down, was teaching the multitudes out of the boat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he left off speaking, he said unto Simon, 'Put back to the deep, and let down your nets for a draught;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Simon answering said to him, 'Master, through the whole night, having laboured, we have taken nothing, but at thy saying I will let down the net.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having done this, they enclosed a great multitude of fishes, and their net was breaking,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they beckoned to the partners, who [are] in the other boat, having come, to help them; and they came, and filled both the boats, so that they were sinking.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Simon Peter having seen, fell down at the knees of Jesus, saying, 'Depart from me, because I am a sinful man, O lord;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for astonishment seized him, and all those with him, at the draught of the fishes that they took,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in like manner also James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon; and Jesus said unto Simon, 'Fear not, henceforth thou shalt be catching men;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having brought the boats upon the land, having left all, did follow him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his being in one of the cities, that lo, a man full of leprosy, and having seen Jesus, having fallen on [his] face, he besought him, saying, 'Sir, if thou mayest will, thou art able to cleanse me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having stretched forth [his] hand, he touched him, having said, 'I will; be thou cleansed;' and immediately the leprosy went away from him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he charged him to tell no one, 'But, having gone away, shew thyself to the priest, and bring near for thy cleansing according as Moses directed, for a testimony to them;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the more was the report going abroad concerning him, and great multitudes were coming together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was withdrawing himself in the desert places and was praying.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that he was teaching, and there were sitting by Pharisees and teachers of the Law, who were come out of every village of Galilee, and Judea, and Jerusalem, and the power of the Lord was -- to heal them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, men bearing upon a couch a man, who hath been struck with palsy, and they were seeking to bring him in, and to place before him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not having found by what way they may bring him in because of the multitude, having gone up on the house-top, through the tiles they let him down, with the little couch, into the midst before Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having seen their faith, said to him, 'Man, thy sins have been forgiven thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, 'Who is this that doth speak evil words? who is able to forgive sins, except God only?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having known their reasonings, answering, said unto them, 'What reason ye in your hearts?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which is easier -- to say, Thy sins have been forgiven thee? or to say, Arise, and walk?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that ye may know that the Son of Man hath authority upon the earth to forgive sins -- (he said to the one struck with palsy) -- I say to thee, Arise, and having taken up thy little couch, be going on to thy house.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And presently having risen before them, having taken up [that] on which he was lying, he went away to his house, glorifying God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and astonishment took all, and they were glorifying God, and were filled with fear, saying -- 'We saw strange things to-day.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after these things he went forth, and beheld a tax-gatherer, by name Levi, sitting at the tax-office, and said to him, 'Be following me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he, having left all, having arisen, did follow him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Levi made a great entertainment to him in his house, and there was a great multitude of tax-gatherers and others who were with them reclining (at meat),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the scribes and the Pharisees among them were murmuring at his disciples, saying, 'Wherefore with tax-gatherers and sinners do ye eat and drink?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said unto them, 'They who are well have no need of a physician, but they that are ill:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I came not to call righteous men, but sinners, to reformation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said unto him, 'Wherefore do the disciples of John fast often, and make supplications -- in like manner also those of the Pharisees -- but thine do eat and drink?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Are ye able to make the sons of the bride-chamber -- in the bridegroom being with them -- to fast?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but days will come, and, when the bridegroom may be taken away from them, then they shall fast in those days.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake also a simile unto them -- 'No one a patch of new clothing doth put on old clothing, and if otherwise, the new also doth make a rent, and with the old the patch doth not agree, that [is] from the new.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And no one doth put new wine into old skins, and if otherwise, the new wine will burst the skins, and itself will be poured out, and the skins will be destroyed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but new wine into new skins is to be put, and both are preserved together;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and no one having drunk old [wine], doth immediately wish new, for he saith, The old is better.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on the second-first sabbath, as he is going through the corn fields, that his disciples were plucking the ears, and were eating, rubbing with the hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain of the Pharisees said to them, 'Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbaths?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said unto them, 'Did ye not read even this that David did, when he hungered, himself and those who are with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how he went into the house of God, and the loaves of the presentation did take, and did eat, and gave also to those with him, which it is not lawful to eat, except only to the priests?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, -- 'The Son of Man is lord also of the sabbath.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass also, on another sabbath, that he goeth into the synagogue, and teacheth, and there was there a man, and his right hand was withered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the scribes and the Pharisees were watching him, if on the sabbath he will heal, that they might find an accusation against him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he himself had known their reasonings, and said to the man having the withered hand, 'Rise, and stand in the midst;' and he having risen, stood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said Jesus unto them, 'I will question you something: Is it lawful on the sabbaths to do good, or to do evil? life to save or to kill?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having looked round on them all, he said to the man, 'Stretch forth thy hand;' and he did so, and his hand was restored whole as the other;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were filled with madness, and were speaking with one another what they might do to Jesus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days, he went forth to the mountain to pray, and was passing the night in the prayer of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when it became day, he called near his disciples, and having chosen from them twelve, whom also he named apostles,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (Simon, whom also he named Peter, and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Matthew and Thomas, James of Alphaeus, and Simon called Zelotes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judas of James, and Judas Iscariot, who also became betrayer;)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come down with them, he stood upon a level spot, and a crowd of his disciples, and a great multitude of the people from all Judea, and Jerusalem, and the maritime Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him, and to be healed of their sicknesses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those harassed by unclean spirits, and they were healed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the multitude were seeking to touch him, because power from him was going forth, and he was healing all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, having lifted up his eyes to his disciples, said: 'Happy the poor -- because yours is the reign of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy those hungering now -- because ye shall be filled. 'Happy those weeping now -- because ye shall laugh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy are ye when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you, and shall reproach, and shall cast forth your name as evil, for the Son of Man's sake --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rejoice in that day, and leap, for lo, your reward [is] great in the heaven, for according to these things were their fathers doing to the prophets.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But woe to you -- the rich, because ye have got your comfort.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you who have been filled -- because ye shall hunger. 'Woe to you who are laughing now -- because ye shall mourn and weep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you when all men shall speak well of you -- for according to these things were their fathers doing to false prophets.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But I say to you who are hearing, Love your enemies, do good to those hating you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: bless those cursing you, and pray for those accusing you falsely;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to him smiting thee upon the cheek, give also the other, and from him taking away from thee the mantle, also the coat thou mayest not keep back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And to every one who is asking of thee, be giving; and from him who is taking away thy goods, be not asking again;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as ye wish that men may do to you, do ye also to them in like manner;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and -- if ye love those loving you, what grace have ye? for also the sinful love those loving them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye do good to those doing good to you, what grace have ye? for also the sinful do the same;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye lend [to those] of whom ye hope to receive back, what grace have ye? for also the sinful lend to sinners -- that they may receive again as much.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again, and your reward will be great, and ye shall be sons of the Highest, because He is kind unto the ungracious and evil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be ye therefore merciful, as also your Father is merciful.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And judge not, and ye may not be judged; condemn not, and ye may not be condemned; release, and ye shall be released.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Give, and it shall be given to you; good measure, pressed, and shaken, and running over, they shall give into your bosom; for with that measure with which ye measure, it shall be measured to you again.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake a simile to them, 'Is blind able to lead blind? shall they not both fall into a pit?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but every one perfected shall be as his teacher.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And why dost thou behold the mote that is in thy brother's eye, and the beam that [is] in thine own eye dost not consider?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or how art thou able to say to thy brother, Brother, suffer, I may take out the mote that [is] in thine eye -- thyself the beam in thine own eye not beholding? Hypocrite, take first the beam out of thine own eye, and then thou shalt see clearly to take out the mote that [is] in thy brother's eye.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For there is not a good tree making bad fruit, nor a bad tree making good fruit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for each tree from its own fruit is known, for not from thorns do they gather figs, nor from a bramble do they crop a grape.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The good man out of the good treasure of his heart doth bring forth that which [is] good; and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart doth bring forth that which [is] evil; for out of the abounding of the heart doth his mouth speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And why do ye call me, Lord, Lord, and do not what I say?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every one who is coming unto me, and is hearing my words, and is doing them, I will shew you to whom he is like;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he is like to a man building a house, who did dig, and deepen, and laid a foundation upon the rock, and a flood having come, the stream broke forth on that house, and was not able to shake it, for it had been founded upon the rock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he who heard and did not, is like to a man having builded a house upon the earth, without a foundation, against which the stream brake forth, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house became great.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he completed all his sayings in the ears of the people, he went into Capernaum;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain centurion's servant being ill, was about to die, who was much valued by him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard about Jesus, he sent unto him elders of the Jews, beseeching him, that having come he might thoroughly save his servant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they, having come near unto Jesus, were calling upon him earnestly, saying -- 'He is worthy to whom thou shalt do this,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he doth love our nation, and the synagogue he did build to us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus was going on with them, and now when he is not far distant from the house the centurion sent unto him friends, saying to him, 'Sir, be not troubled, for I am not worthy that under my roof thou mayest enter;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: wherefore not even myself thought I worthy to come unto thee, but say in a word, and my lad shall be healed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers, and I say to this [one], Go, and he goeth; and to another, Be coming, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doth [it].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having heard these things Jesus wondered at him, and having turned to the multitude following him, he said, 'I say to you, not even in Israel so much faith did I find;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those sent, having turned back to the house, found the ailing servant in health.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on the morrow, he was going on to a city called Nain, and there were going with him many of his disciples, and a great multitude,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as he came nigh to the gate of the city, then, lo, one dead was being carried forth, an only son of his mother, and she a widow, and a great multitude of the city was with her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord having seen her, was moved with compassion towards her, and said to her, 'Be not weeping;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come near, he touched the bier, and those bearing [it] stood still, and he said, 'Young man, to thee I say, Arise;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the dead sat up, and began to speak, and he gave him to his mother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and fear took hold of all, and they were glorifying God, saying -- 'A great prophet hath risen among us,' and -- 'God did look upon His people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the account of this went forth in all Judea about him, and in all the region around.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the disciples of John told him about all these things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and John having called near a certain two of his disciples, sent unto Jesus, saying, 'Art thou he who is coming, or for another do we look?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come near to him, the men said, 'John the Baptist sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he who is coming, or for another do we look?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in that hour he cured many from sicknesses, and plagues, and evil spirits, and to many blind he granted sight.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Having gone on, report to John what ye saw and heard, that blind men do see again, lame do walk, lepers are cleansed, deaf do hear, dead are raised, poor have good news proclaimed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and happy is he whoever may not be stumbled in me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messengers of John having gone away, he began to say unto the multitudes concerning John: 'What have ye gone forth to the wilderness to look on? a reed by the wind shaken?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but what have ye gone forth to see? a man in soft garments clothed? lo, they in splendid apparellings, and living in luxury, are in the houses of kings!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But what have ye gone forth to see? a prophet? Yes, I say to you, and much more than a prophet:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this is he concerning whom it hath been written, Lo, I send my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, a greater prophet, among those born of women, than John the Baptist there is not; but the least in the reign of God is greater than he.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the people having heard, and the tax-gatherers, declared God righteous, having been baptized with the baptism of John,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the Pharisees, and the lawyers, the counsel of God did put away for themselves, not having been baptized by him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said, 'To what, then, shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they are like to children, to those sitting in a market-place, and calling one to another, and saying, We piped to you, and ye did not dance, we mourned to you, and ye did not weep!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and ye say, He hath a demon;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Son of Man came eating and drinking, and ye say, Lo, a man, a glutton, and a wine drinker, a friend of tax-gatherers and sinners;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the wisdom was justified from all her children.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain one of the Pharisees was asking him that he might eat with him, and having gone into the house of the Pharisee he reclined (at meat),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a woman in the city, who was a sinner, having known that he reclineth (at meat) in the house of the Pharisee, having provided an alabaster box of ointment,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having stood behind, beside his feet, weeping, she began to wet his feet with the tears, and with the hairs of her head she was wiping, and was kissing his feet, and was anointing with the ointment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Pharisee who did call him, having seen, spake within himself, saying, 'This one, if he were a prophet, would have known who and of what kind [is] the woman who doth touch him, that she is a sinner.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said unto him, 'Simon, I have something to say to thee;' and he saith, 'Teacher, say on.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Two debtors were to a certain creditor; the one was owing five hundred denaries, and the other fifty;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they not having [wherewith] to give back, he forgave both; which then of them, say thou, will love him more?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Simon answering said, 'I suppose that to whom he forgave the more;' and he said to him, 'Rightly thou didst judge.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having turned unto the woman, he said to Simon, 'Seest thou this woman? I entered into thy house; water for my feet thou didst not give, but this woman with tears did wet my feet, and with the hairs of her head did wipe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a kiss to me thou didst not give, but this woman, from what [time] I came in, did not cease kissing my feet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with oil my head thou didst not anoint, but this woman with ointment did anoint my feet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore I say to thee, her many sins have been forgiven, because she did love much; but to whom little is forgiven, little he doth love.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to her, 'Thy sins have been forgiven;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those reclining with him (at meat) began to say within themselves, 'Who is this, who also doth forgive sins?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto the woman, 'Thy faith have saved thee, be going on to peace.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass thereafter, that he was going through every city and village, preaching and proclaiming good news of the reign of God, and the twelve [are] with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain women, who were healed of evil spirits and infirmities, Mary who is called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone forth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joanna wife of Chuza, steward of Herod, and Susanna, and many others, who were ministering to him from their substance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a great multitude having gathered, and those who from city and city were coming unto him, he spake by a simile:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The sower went forth to sow his seed, and in his sowing some indeed fell beside the way, and it was trodden down, and the fowls of the heaven did devour it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And other fell upon the rock, and having sprung up, it did wither, through not having moisture.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And other fell amidst the thorns, and the thorns having sprung up with it, did choke it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And other fell upon the good ground, and having sprung up, it made fruit an hundred fold.' These things saying, he was calling, 'He having ears to hear -- let him hear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples were questioning him, saying, 'What may this simile be?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'To you it hath been given to know the secrets of the reign of God, and to the rest in similes; that seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this is the simile: The seed is the word of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those beside the way are those hearing, then cometh the Devil, and taketh up the word from their heart, lest having believed, they may be saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And those upon the rock: They who, when they may hear, with joy do receive the word, and these have no root, who for a time believe, and in time of temptation fall away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that which fell to the thorns: These are they who have heard, and going forth, through anxieties, and riches, and pleasures of life, are choked, and bear not to completion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that in the good ground: These are they, who in an upright and good heart, having heard the word, do retain [it], and bear fruit in continuance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And no one having lighted a lamp doth cover it with a vessel, or under a couch doth put [it]; but upon a lamp-stand he doth put [it], that those coming in may see the light,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for nothing is secret, that shall not become manifest, nor hid, that shall not be known, and become manifest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'See, therefore, how ye hear, for whoever may have, there shall be given to him, and whoever may not have, also what he seemeth to have, shall be taken from him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came unto him his mother and brethren, and they were not able to get to him because of the multitude,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it was told him, saying, 'Thy mother and thy brethren do stand without, wishing to see thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said unto them, 'My mother and my brethren! they are those who the word of God are hearing, and doing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on one of the days, that he himself went into a boat with his disciples, and he said unto them, 'We may go over to the other side of the lake;' and they set forth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as they are sailing he fell deeply asleep, and there came down a storm of wind to the lake, and they were filling, and were in peril.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come near, they awoke him, saying, 'Master, master, we perish;' and he, having arisen, rebuked the wind and the raging of the water, and they ceased, and there came a calm,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Where is your faith?' and they being afraid did wonder, saying unto one another, 'Who, then, is this, that even the winds he doth command, and the water, and they obey him?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they sailed down to the region of the Gadarenes, that is over-against Galilee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having gone forth upon the land, there met him a certain man, out of the city, who had demons for a long time, and with a garment was not clothed, and in a house was not abiding, but in the tombs,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen Jesus, and having cried out, he fell before him, and with a loud voice, said, 'What -- to me and to thee, Jesus, Son of God Most High? I beseech thee, mayest thou not afflict me!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he commanded the unclean spirit to come forth from the man, for many times it had caught him, and he was being bound with chains and fetters -- guarded, and breaking asunder the bonds he was driven by the demons to the deserts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus questioned him, saying, 'What is thy name?' and he said, 'Legion,' (because many demons were entered into him,)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was calling on him, that he may not command them to go away to the abyss,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there was there a herd of many swine feeding in the mountain, and they were calling on him, that he might suffer them to enter into these, and he suffered them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the demons having gone forth from the man, did enter into the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep to the lake, and were choked.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those feeding [them], having seen what was come to pass, fled, and having gone, told [it] to the city, and to the fields;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they came forth to see what was come to pass, and they came unto Jesus, and found the man sitting, out of whom the demons had gone forth, clothed, and right-minded, at the feet of Jesus, and they were afraid;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those also having seen [it], told them how the demoniac was saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the whole multitude of the region of the Gadarenes round about asked him to go away from them, because with great fear they were pressed, and he having entered into the boat, did turn back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the man from whom the demons had gone forth was beseeching of him to be with him, and Jesus sent him away, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Turn back to thy house, and tell how great things God did to thee;' and he went away through all the city proclaiming how great things Jesus did to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in the turning back of Jesus, the multitude received him, for they were all looking for him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there came a man, whose name [is] Jairus, and he was a chief of the synagogue, and having fallen at the feet of Jesus, was calling on him to come to his house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because he had an only daughter about twelve years [old], and she was dying. And in his going away, the multitudes were thronging him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a woman, having an issue of blood for twelve years, who, having spent on physicians all her living, was not able to be healed by any,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having come near behind, touched the fringe of his garment, and presently the issue of her blood stood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said, 'Who [is] it that touched me?' and all denying, Peter and those with him said, 'Master, the multitudes press thee, and throng [thee], and thou dost say, Who [is] it that touched me!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said, 'Some one did touch me, for I knew power having gone forth from me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the woman, having seen that she was not hid, trembling, came, and having fallen before him, for what cause she touched him declared to him before all the people, and how she was healed presently;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to her, 'Take courage, daughter, thy faith hath saved thee, be going on to peace.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While he is yet speaking, there doth come a certain one from the chief of the synagogue's [house], saying to him -- 'Thy daughter hath died, harass not the Teacher;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having heard, answered him, saying, 'Be not afraid, only believe, and she shall be saved.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come to the house, he suffered no one to go in, except Peter, and James, and John, and the father of the child, and the mother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were all weeping, and beating themselves for her, and he said, 'Weep not, she did not die, but doth sleep;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were deriding him, knowing that she did die;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having put all forth without, and having taken hold of her hand, called, saying, 'Child, arise;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her spirit came back, and she arose presently, and he directed that there be given to her to eat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and her parents were amazed, but he charged them to say to no one what was come to pass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having called together his twelve disciples, he gave them power and authority over all the demons, and to cure sicknesses,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sent them to proclaim the reign of God, and to heal the ailing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Take nothing for the way, neither staff, nor scrip, nor bread, nor money; neither have two coats each;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and into whatever house ye may enter, there remain, and thence depart;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as many as may not receive you, going forth from that city, even the dust from your feet shake off, for a testimony against them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And going forth they were going through the several villages, proclaiming good news, and healing everywhere.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Herod the tetrarch heard of all the things being done by him, and was perplexed, because it was said by certain, that John hath been raised out of the dead;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and by certain, that Elijah did appear, and by others, that a prophet, one of the ancients, was risen;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Herod said, 'John I did behead, but who is this concerning whom I hear such things?' and he was seeking to see him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the apostles having turned back, declared to him how great things they did, and having taken them, he withdrew by himself to a desert place of a city called Bethsaida,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the multitudes having known did follow him, and having received them, he was speaking to them concerning the reign of God, and those having need of service he cured.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day began to decline, and the twelve having come near, said to him, 'Let away the multitude, that having gone to the villages and the fields round about, they may lodge and may find provision, because here we are in a desert place.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Give ye them to eat;' and they said, 'We have no more than five loaves, and two fishes: except, having gone, we may buy for all this people victuals;'
