translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for they were about five thousand men. And he said unto his disciples, 'Cause them to recline in companies, in each fifty;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did so, and made all to recline;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken the five loaves, and the two fishes, having looked up to the heaven, he blessed them, and brake, and was giving to the disciples to set before the multitude;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did eat, and were all filled, and there was taken up what was over to them of broken pieces, twelve baskets.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, as he is praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he questioned them, saying, 'Who do the multitudes say me to be?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they answering said, 'John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and others, that a prophet, one of the ancients, was risen;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'And ye -- who do ye say me to be?' and Peter answering said, 'The Christ of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having charged them, he commanded [them] to say this to no one,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- 'It behoveth the Son of Man to suffer many things, and to be rejected by the elders, and chief priests, and scribes, and to be killed, and the third day to be raised.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto all, 'If any one doth will to come after me, let him disown himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for whoever may will to save his life, shall lose it, and whoever may lose his life for my sake, he shall save it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for what is a man profited, having gained the whole world, and having lost or having forfeited himself?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For whoever may be ashamed of me, and of my words, of this one shall the Son of Man be ashamed, when he may come in his glory, and the Father's, and the holy messengers';

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, truly, there are certain of those here standing, who shall not taste of death till they may see the reign of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, after these words, as it were eight days, that having taken Peter, and John, and James, he went up to the mountain to pray,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it came to pass, in his praying, the appearance of his face became altered, and his garment white -- sparkling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, two men were speaking together with him, who were Moses and Elijah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who having appeared in glory, spake of his outgoing that he was about to fulfil in Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but Peter and those with him were heavy with sleep, and having waked, they saw his glory, and the two men standing with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in their parting from him, Peter said unto Jesus, 'Master, it is good to us to be here; and we may make three booths, one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah,' not knowing what he saith:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as he was speaking these things, there came a cloud, and overshadowed them, and they feared in their entering into the cloud,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a voice came out of the cloud saying, 'This is My Son -- the Beloved; hear ye him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when the voice was past, Jesus was found alone; and they were silent, and declared to no one in those days anything of what they have seen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass on the next day, they having come down from the mount, there met him a great multitude,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a man from the multitude cried out, saying, 'Teacher, I beseech thee, look upon my son, because he is my only begotten;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a spirit doth take him, and suddenly he doth cry out, and it teareth him, with foaming, and it hardly departeth from him, bruising him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I besought thy disciples that they might cast it out, and they were not able.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, 'O generation, unstedfast and perverse, till when shall I be with you, and suffer you? bring near hither thy son;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as he is yet coming near, the demon rent him, and tore [him] sore, and Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the youth, and gave him back to his father.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they were all amazed at the greatness of God, and while all are wondering at all things that Jesus did, he said unto his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lay ye to your ears these words, for the Son of Man is about to be delivered up to the hands of men.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they were not knowing this saying, and it was veiled from them, that they might not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there entered a reasoning among them, this, Who may be greater of them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having seen the reasoning of their heart, having taken hold of a child, set him beside himself,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and said to them, 'Whoever may receive this child in my name, doth receive me, and whoever may receive me, doth receive Him who sent me, for he who is least among you all -- he shall be great.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And John answering said, 'Master, we saw a certain one in thy name casting forth the demons, and we forbade him, because he doth not follow with us;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said unto him, 'Forbid not, for he who is not against us, is for us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in the completing of the days of his being taken up, that he fixed his face to go on to Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sent messengers before his face, and having gone on, they went into a village of Samaritans, to make ready for him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did not receive him, because his face was going on to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples James and John having seen, said, 'Sir, wilt thou [that] we may command fire to come down from the heaven, and to consume them, as also Elijah did?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having turned, he rebuked them, and said, 'Ye have not known of what spirit ye are;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Son of Man did not come to destroy men's lives, but to save;' and they went on to another village.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, as they are going on in the way, a certain one said unto him, 'I will follow thee wherever thou mayest go, sir;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'The foxes have holes, and the fowls of the heaven places of rest, but the Son of Man hath not where he may recline the head.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto another, 'Be following me;' and he said, 'Sir, permit me, having gone away, first to bury my father;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'Suffer the dead to bury their own dead, and thou, having gone away, publish the reign of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And another also said, 'I will follow thee, sir, but first permit me to take leave of those in my house;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said unto him, 'No one having put his hand on a plough, and looking back, is fit for the reign of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after these things, the Lord did appoint also other seventy, and sent them by twos before his face, to every city and place whither he himself was about to come,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then said he unto them, 'The harvest indeed [is] abundant, but the workmen few; beseech ye then the Lord of the harvest, that He may put forth workmen to His harvest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Go away; lo, I send you forth as lambs in the midst of wolves;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: carry no bag, no scrip, nor sandals; and salute no one on the way;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and into whatever house ye do enter, first say, Peace to this house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if indeed there may be there the son of peace, rest on it shall your peace; and if not so, upon you it shall turn back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And in that house remain, eating and drinking the things they have, for worthy [is] the workman of his hire; go not from house to house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and into whatever city ye enter, and they may receive you, eat the things set before you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and heal the ailing in it, and say to them, The reign of God hath come nigh to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And into whatever city ye do enter, and they may not receive you, having gone forth to its broad places, say,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the dust that hath cleaved to us, from your city, we do wipe off against you, but this know ye, that the reign of God hath come nigh to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, that for Sodom in that day it shall be more tolerable than for that city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to thee, Chorazin; woe to thee, Bethsaida; for if in Tyre and Sidon had been done the mighty works that were done in you, long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes, they had reformed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but for Tyre and Sidon it shall be more tolerable in the judgment than for you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou, Capernaum, which unto the heaven wast exalted, unto hades thou shalt be brought down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is hearing you, doth hear me; and he who is putting you away, doth put me away; and he who is putting me away, doth put away Him who sent me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the seventy turned back with joy, saying, 'Sir, and the demons are being subjected to us in thy name;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'I was beholding the Adversary, as lightning from the heaven having fallen;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lo, I give to you the authority to tread upon serpents and scorpions, and on all the power of the enemy, and nothing by any means shall hurt you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subjected to you, but rejoice rather that your names were written in the heavens.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In that hour was Jesus glad in the Spirit, and said, 'I do confess to thee, Father, Lord of the heaven and of the earth, that Thou didst hide these things from wise men and understanding, and didst reveal them to babes; yes, Father, because so it became good pleasure before Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'All things were delivered up to me by my Father, and no one doth know who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whom the Son may wish to reveal [Him].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having turned unto the disciples, he said, by themselves, 'Happy the eyes that are perceiving what ye perceive;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, that many prophets and kings did wish to see what ye perceive, and did not see, and to hear what ye hear, and did not hear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, a certain lawyer stood up, trying him, and saying, 'Teacher, what having done, life age-during shall I inherit?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto him, 'In the law what hath been written? how dost thou read?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said, 'Thou shalt love the Lord thy God out of all thy heart, and out of all thy soul, and out of all thy strength, and out of all thy understanding, and thy neighbour as thyself.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to him, 'Rightly thou didst answer; this do, and thou shalt live.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, willing to declare himself righteous, said unto Jesus, 'And who is my neighbour?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having taken up [the word], said, 'A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among robbers, and having stripped him and inflicted blows, they went away, leaving [him] half dead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And by a coincidence a certain priest was going down in that way, and having seen him, he passed over on the opposite side;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in like manner also, a Levite, having been about the place, having come and seen, passed over on the opposite side.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But a certain Samaritan, journeying, came along him, and having seen him, he was moved with compassion,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come near, he bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine, and having lifted him up on his own beast, he brought him to an inn, and was careful of him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the morrow, going forth, taking out two denaries, he gave to the innkeeper, and said to him, Be careful of him, and whatever thou mayest spend more, I, in my coming again, will give back to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who, then, of these three, seemeth to thee to have become neighbour of him who fell among the robbers?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'He who did the kindness with him,' then Jesus said to him, 'Be going on, and thou be doing in like manner.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in their going on, that he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman, by name Martha, did receive him into her house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she had also a sister, called Mary, who also, having seated herself beside the feet of Jesus, was hearing the word,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Martha was distracted about much serving, and having stood by him, she said, 'Sir, dost thou not care that my sister left me alone to serve? say then to her, that she may partake along with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to her, 'Martha, Martha, thou art anxious and disquieted about many things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but of one thing there is need, and Mary the good part did choose, that shall not be taken away from her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his being in a certain place praying, as he ceased, a certain one of his disciples said unto him, 'Sir, teach us to pray, as also John taught his disciples.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'When ye may pray, say ye: Our Father who art in the heavens; hallowed be Thy name: Thy reign come; Thy will come to pass, as in heaven also on earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: our appointed bread be giving us daily;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and forgive us our sins, for also we ourselves forgive every one indebted to us; and mayest Thou not bring us into temptation; but do Thou deliver us from the evil.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Who of you shall have a friend, and shall go on unto him at midnight, and may say to him, Friend, lend me three loaves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: seeing a friend of mine came out of the way unto me, and I have not what I shall set before him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he from within answering may say, Do not give me trouble, already the door hath been shut, and my children with me are in the bed, I am not able, having risen, to give to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I say to you, even if he will not give to him, having risen, because of his being his friend, yet because of his importunity, having risen, he will give him as many as he doth need;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for every one who is asking doth receive; and he who is seeking doth find; and to him who is knocking it shall be opened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And of which of you -- the father -- if the son shall ask a loaf, a stone will he present to him? and if a fish, will he instead of a fish, a serpent present to him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he may ask an egg, will he present to him a scorpion?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If, then, ye, being evil, have known good gifts to be giving to your children, how much more shall the Father who is from heaven give the Holy Spirit to those asking Him!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was casting forth a demon, and it was dumb, and it came to pass, the demon having gone forth, the dumb man spake, and the multitudes wondered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain of them said, 'By Beelzeboul, ruler of the demons, he doth cast forth the demons;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and others, tempting, a sign out of heaven from him were asking.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, 'Every kingdom having been divided against itself is desolated; and house against house doth fall;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if also the Adversary against himself was divided, how shall his kingdom be made to stand? for ye say, by Beelzeboul is my casting forth the demons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But if I by Beelzeboul cast forth the demons -- your sons, by whom do they cast forth? because of this your judges they shall be;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but if by the finger of God I cast forth the demons, then come unawares upon you did the reign of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When the strong man armed may keep his hall, in peace are his goods;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but when the stronger than he, having come upon [him], may overcome him, his whole-armour he doth take away in which he had trusted, and his spoils he distributeth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he who is not with me is against me, and he who is not gathering with me doth scatter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When the unclean spirit may go forth from the man it walketh through waterless places seeking rest, and not finding, it saith, I will turn back to my house whence I came forth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come, it findeth [it] swept and adorned;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then doth it go, and take to it seven other spirits more evil than itself, and having entered, they dwell there, and the last of that man becometh worst than the first.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his saying these things, a certain woman having lifted up the voice out of the multitude, said to him, 'Happy the womb that carried thee, and the paps that thou didst suck!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'Yea, rather, happy those hearing the word of God, and keeping [it]!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitudes crowding together upon him, he began to say, 'This generation is evil, a sign it doth seek after, and a sign shall not be given to it, except the sign of Jonah the prophet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so also shall the Son of Man be to this generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and shall condemn them, because she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and lo, greater than Solomon here!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it, because they reformed at the proclamation of Jonah; and lo, greater than Jonah here!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And no one having lighted a lamp, doth put [it] in a secret place, nor under the measure, but on the lamp-stand, that those coming in may behold the light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The lamp of the body is the eye, when then thine eye may be simple, thy whole body also is lightened; and when it may be evil, thy body also is darkened;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: take heed, then, lest the light that [is] in thee be darkness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if then thy whole body is lightened, not having any part darkened, the whole shall be lightened, as when the lamp by the brightness may give thee light.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in [his] speaking, a certain Pharisee was asking him that he might dine with him, and having gone in, he reclined (at meat),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pharisee having seen, did wonder that he did not first baptize himself before the dinner.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said unto him, 'Now do ye, the Pharisees, the outside of the cup and of the plate make clean, but your inward part is full of rapine and wickedness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: unthinking! did not He who made the outside also the inside make?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But what ye have give ye [as] alms, and, lo, all things are clean to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But woe to you, the Pharisees, because ye tithe the mint, and the rue, and every herb, and ye pass by the judgment, and the love of God; these things it behoveth to do, and those not to be neglecting.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, the Pharisees, because ye love the first seats in the synagogues, and the salutations in the market-places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, because ye are as the unseen tombs, and the men walking above have not known.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And one of the lawyers answering, saith to him, 'Teacher, these things saying, us also thou dost insult;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'And to you, the lawyers, woe! because ye burden men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves with one of your fingers do not touch the burdens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then do ye testify, and are well pleased with the works of your fathers, because they indeed killed them, and ye do build their tombs;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of this also the wisdom of God said: I will send to them prophets, and apostles, and some of them they shall kill and persecute,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the blood of all the prophets, that is being poured forth from the foundation of the world, may be required from this generation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, who perished between the altar and the house; yes, I say to you, It shall be required from this generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, the lawyers, because ye took away the key of the knowledge; yourselves ye did not enter; and those coming in, ye did hinder.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in his speaking these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began fearfully to urge and to press him to speak about many things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At which time the myriads of the multitude having been gathered together, so as to tread upon one another, he began to say unto his disciples, first, 'Take heed to yourselves of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because whatever in the darkness ye said, in the light shall be heard: and what to the ear ye spake in the inner-chambers, shall be proclaimed upon the house-tops.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say to you, my friends, be not afraid of those killing the body, and after these things are not having anything over to do;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I will show to you, whom ye may fear; Fear him who, after the killing, is having authority to cast to the gehenna; yes, I say to you, Fear ye Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Are not five sparrows sold for two assars? and one of them is not forgotten before God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but even the hairs of your head have been all numbered; therefore fear ye not, than many sparrows ye are of more value.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say to you, Every one -- whoever may confess with me before men, the Son of Man also shall confess with him before the messengers of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who hath denied me before men, shall be denied before the messengers of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every one whoever shall say a word to the Son of Man, it shall be forgiven to him, but to him who to the Holy Spirit did speak evil, it shall not be forgiven.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when they bring you before the synagogues, and the rulers, and the authorities, be not anxious how or what ye may reply, or what ye may say,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Holy Spirit shall teach you in that hour what it behoveth [you] to say.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain one said to him, out of the multitude, 'Teacher, say to my brother to divide with me the inheritance.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to him, 'Man, who set me a judge or a divider over you?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'Observe, and beware of the covetousness, because not in the abundance of one's goods is his life.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake a simile unto them, saying, 'Of a certain rich man the field brought forth well;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was reasoning within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have not where I shall gather together my fruits?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, This I will do, I will take down my storehouses, and greater ones I will build, and I will gather together there all my products and my good things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast many good things laid up for many years, be resting, eat, drink, be merry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And God said to him, Unthinking one! this night thy soul they shall require from thee, and what things thou didst prepare -- to whom shall they be?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so [is] he who is treasuring up to himself, and is not rich toward God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto his disciples, 'Because of this, to you I say, Be not anxious for your life, what ye may eat; nor for the body, what ye may put on;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the life is more than the nourishment, and the body than the clothing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Consider the ravens, that they sow not, nor reap, to which there is no barn nor storehouse, and God doth nourish them; how much better are ye than the fowls?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and who of you, being anxious, is able to add to his age one cubit?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If, then, ye are not able for the least -- why for the rest are ye anxious?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Consider the lilies, how do they grow? they labour not, nor do they spin, and I say to you, not even Solomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the herbage in the field, that to-day is, and to-morrow into an oven is cast, God doth so clothe, how much more you -- ye of little faith?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye -- seek not what ye may eat, or what ye may drink, and be not in suspense,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all these things do the nations of the world seek after, and your Father hath known that ye have need of these things;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, seek ye the reign of God, and all these things shall be added to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Fear not, little flock, because your Father did delight to give you the reign;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sell your goods, and give alms, make to yourselves bags that become not old, a treasure unfailing in the heavens, where thief doth not come near, nor moth destroy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for where your treasure is, there also your heart will be.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Let your loins be girded, and the lamps burning,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye like to men waiting for their lord, when he shall return out of the wedding feasts, that he having come and knocked, immediately they may open to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy those servants, whom the lord, having come, shall find watching; verily I say to you, that he will gird himself, and will cause them to recline (at meat), and having come near, will minister to them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he may come in the second watch, and in the third watch he may come, and may find [it] so, happy are those servants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And this know, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief doth come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken through;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye, then, become ye ready, because at the hour ye think not, the Son of Man doth come.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter said to him, 'Sir, unto us this simile dost thou speak, or also unto all?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said, 'Who, then, is the faithful and prudent steward whom the lord shall set over his household, to give in season the wheat measure?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Happy that servant, whom his lord, having come, shall find doing so;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: truly I say to you, that over all his goods he will set him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if that servant may say in his heart, My lord doth delay to come, and may begin to beat the men-servants and the maid-servants, to eat also, and to drink, and to be drunken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the lord of that servant will come in a day in which he doth not look for [him], and in an hour that he doth not know, and will cut him off, and his portion with the unfaithful he will appoint.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that servant, who having known his lord's will, and not having prepared, nor having gone according to his will, shall be beaten with many stripes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who, not having known, and having done things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few; and to every one to whom much was given, much shall be required from him; and to whom they did commit much, more abundantly they will ask of him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Fire I came to cast to the earth, and what will I if already it was kindled?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how am I pressed till it may be completed!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Think ye that peace I came to give in the earth? no, I say to you, but rather division;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there shall be henceforth five in one house divided -- three against two, and two against three;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a father shall be divided against a son, and a son against a father, a mother against a daughter, and a daughter against a mother, a mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said also to the multitudes, 'When ye may see the cloud rising from the west, immediately ye say, A shower doth come, and it is so;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when -- a south wind blowing, ye say, that there will be heat, and it is;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: hypocrites! the face of the earth and of the heaven ye have known to make proof of, but this time -- how do ye not make proof of [it]?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And why, also, of yourselves, judge ye not what is righteous?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, as thou art going away with thy opponent to the ruler, in the way give diligence to be released from him, lest he may drag thee unto the judge, and the judge may deliver thee to the officer, and the officer may cast thee into prison;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say to thee, thou mayest not come forth thence till even the last mite thou mayest give back.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were present certain at that time, telling him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate did mingle with their sacrifices;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus answering said to them, 'Think ye that these Galileans became sinners beyond all the Galileans, because they have suffered such things?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: No -- I say to you, but, if ye may not reform, all ye even so shall perish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell, and killed them; think ye that these became debtors beyond all men who are dwelling in Jerusalem?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: No -- I say to you, but, if ye may not reform, all ye in like manner shall perish.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake this simile: 'A certain one had a fig-tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit in it, and he did not find;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto the vine-dresser, Lo, three years I come seeking fruit in this fig-tree, and do not find, cut it off, why also the ground doth it render useless?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he answering saith to him, Sir, suffer it also this year, till that I may dig about it, and cast in dung;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if indeed it may bear fruit -- ;and if not so, thereafter thou shalt cut it off.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there was a woman having a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and she was bowed together, and not able to bend back at all,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having seen her, did call [her] near, and said to her, 'Woman, thou hast been loosed from thy infirmity;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he laid on her [his] hands, and presently she was set upright, and was glorifying God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief of the synagogue answering -- much displeased that on the sabbath Jesus healed -- said to the multitude, 'Six days there are in which it behoveth [us] to be working; in these, then, coming, be healed, and not on the sabbath-day.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then the Lord answered him and said, 'Hypocrite, doth not each of you on the sabbath loose his ox or ass from the stall, and having led away, doth water [it]?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this one, being a daughter of Abraham, whom the Adversary bound, lo, eighteen years, did it not behove to be loosed from this bond on the sabbath-day?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saying these things, all who were opposed to him were being ashamed, and all the multitude were rejoicing over all the glorious things that are being done by him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'To what is the reign of God like? and to what shall I liken it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It is like to a grain of mustard, which a man having taken, did cast into his garden, and it increased, and came to a great tree, and the fowls of the heavens did rest in its branches.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And again he said, 'To what shall I liken the reign of God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It is like leaven, which a woman, having taken, did hide in three measures of meal, till that all was leavened.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was going through cities and villages, teaching, and making progress toward Jerusalem;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain one said to him, 'Sir, are those saved few?' and he said unto them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Be striving to go in through the straight gate, because many, I say to you, will seek to go in, and shall not be able;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: from the time the master of the house may have risen up, and may have shut the door, and ye may begin without to stand, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, lord, open to us, and he answering shall say to you, I have not known you whence ye are,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then ye may begin to say, We did eat before thee, and did drink, and in our broad places thou didst teach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall say, I say to you, I have not known you whence ye are; depart from me, all ye workers of the unrighteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'There shall be there the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth, when ye may see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the reign of God, and yourselves being cast out without;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they shall come from east and west, and from north and south, and shall recline in the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there are last who shall be first, and there are first who shall be last.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On that day there came near certain Pharisees, saying to him, 'Go forth, and be going on hence, for Herod doth wish to kill thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Having gone, say to this fox, Lo, I cast forth demons, and perfect cures to-day and to-morrow, and the third [day] I am being perfected;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but it behoveth me to-day, and to-morrow, and the [day] following, to go on, because it is not possible for a prophet to perish out of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that is killing the prophets, and stoning those sent unto her, how often did I will to gather together thy children, as a hen her brood under the wings, and ye did not will.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, your house is being left to you desolate, and verily I say to you -- ye may not see me, till it may come, when ye may say, Blessed [is] he who is coming in the name of the Lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on his going into the house of a certain one of the chiefs of the Pharisees, on a sabbath, to eat bread, that they were watching him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there was a certain dropsical man before him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus answering spake to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, 'Is it lawful on the sabbath-day to heal?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were silent, and having taken hold of [him], he healed him, and let [him] go;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and answering them he said, 'Of which of you shall an ass or ox fall into a pit, and he will not immediately draw it up on the sabbath-day?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were not able to answer him again unto these things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake a simile unto those called, marking how they were choosing out the first couches, saying unto them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'When thou mayest be called by any one to marriage-feasts, thou mayest not recline on the first couch, lest a more honourable than thou may have been called by him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who did call thee and him having come shall say to thee, Give to this one place, and then thou mayest begin with shame to occupy the last place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, when thou mayest be called, having gone on, recline in the last place, that when he who called thee may come, he may say to thee, Friend, come up higher; then thou shalt have glory before those reclining with thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because every one who is exalting himself shall be humbled, and he who is humbling himself shall be exalted.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said also to him who did call him, 'When thou mayest make a dinner or a supper, be not calling thy friends, nor thy brethren, nor thy kindred, nor rich neighbours, lest they may also call thee again, and a recompense may come to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but when thou mayest make a feast, be calling poor, maimed, lame, blind,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and happy thou shalt be, because they have not to recompense thee, for it shall be recompensed to thee in the rising again of the righteous.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And one of those reclining with him, having heard these things, said to him, 'Happy [is] he who shall eat bread in the reign of God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to him, 'A certain man made a great supper, and called many,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sent his servant at the hour of the supper to say to those having been called, Be coming, because now are all things ready.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And they began with one consent all to excuse themselves: The first said to him, A field I bought, and I have need to go forth and see it; I beg of thee, have me excused.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And another said, Five yoke of oxen I bought, and I go on to prove them; I beg of thee, have me excused:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and another said, A wife I married, and because of this I am not able to come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that servant having come, told to his lord these things, then the master of the house, having been angry, said to his servant, Go forth quickly to the broad places and lanes of the city, and the poor, and maimed, and lame, and blind, bring in hither.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the servant said, Sir, it hath been done as thou didst command, and still there is room.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the lord said unto the servant, Go forth to the ways and hedges, and constrain to come in, that my house may be filled;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, that none of those men who have been called shall taste of my supper.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were going on with him great multitudes, and having turned, he said unto them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If any one doth come unto me, and doth not hate his own father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brothers, and sisters, and yet even his own life, he is not able to be my disciple;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, is not able to be my disciple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For who of you, willing to build a tower, doth not first, having sat down, count the expence, whether he have the things for completing?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: lest that he having laid a foundation, and not being able to finish, all who are beholding may begin to mock him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- This man began to build, and was not able to finish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or what king going on to engage with another king in war, doth not, having sat down, first consult if he be able with ten thousand to meet him who with twenty thousand is coming against him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if not so -- he being yet a long way off -- having sent an embassy, he doth ask the things for peace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'So, then, every one of you who doth not take leave of all that he himself hath, is not able to be my disciple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The salt [is] good, but if the salt doth become tasteless, with what shall it be seasoned?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: neither for land nor for manure is it fit -- they cast it without. He who is having ears to hear -- let him hear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the tax-gatherers and the sinners were coming nigh to him, to hear him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pharisees and the scribes were murmuring, saying -- This one doth receive sinners, and doth eat with them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake unto them this simile, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'What man of you having a hundred sheep, and having lost one out of them, doth not leave behind the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go on after the lost one, till he may find it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having found, he doth lay [it] on his shoulders rejoicing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come to the house, he doth call together the friends and the neighbours, saying to them, Rejoice with me, because I found my sheep -- the lost one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I say to you, that so joy shall be in the heaven over one sinner reforming, rather than over ninety-nine righteous men, who have no need of reformation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or what woman having ten drachms, if she may lose one drachm, doth not light a lamp, and sweep the house, and seek carefully till that she may find?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having found, she doth call together the female friends and the neighbours, saying, Rejoice with me, for I found the drachm that I lost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'So I say to you, joy doth come before the messengers of God over one sinner reforming.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'A certain man had two sons,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the younger of them said to the father, Father, give me the portion of the substance falling to [me], and he divided to them the living.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And not many days after, having gathered all together, the younger son went abroad to a far country, and there he scattered his substance, living riotously;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having spent all, there came a mighty famine on that country, and himself began to be in want;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone on, he joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him to the fields to feed swine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was desirous to fill his belly from the husks that the swine were eating, and no one was giving to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having come to himself, he said, How many hirelings of my father have a superabundance of bread, and I here with hunger am perishing!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having risen, I will go on unto my father, and will say to him, Father, I did sin -- to the heaven, and before thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and no more am I worthy to be called thy son; make me as one of thy hirelings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having risen, he went unto his own father, and he being yet far distant, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and having ran he fell upon his neck and kissed him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the son said to him, Father, I did sin -- to the heaven, and before thee, and no more am I worthy to be called thy son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the father said unto his servants, Bring forth the first robe, and clothe him, and give a ring for his hand, and sandals for the feet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having brought the fatted calf, kill [it], and having eaten, we may be merry,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because this my son was dead, and did live again, and he was lost, and was found; and they began to be merry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And his elder son was in a field, and as, coming, he drew nigh to the house, he heard music and dancing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having called near one of the young men, he was inquiring what these things might be,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to him -- Thy brother is arrived, and thy father did kill the fatted calf, because in health he did receive him back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he was angry, and would not go in, therefore his father, having come forth, was entreating him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said to the father, Lo, so many years I do serve thee, and never thy command did I transgress, and to me thou didst never give a kid, that with my friends I might make merry;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but when thy son -- this one who did devour thy living with harlots -- came, thou didst kill to him the fatted calf.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he said to him, Child, thou art always with me, and all my things are thine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but to be merry, and to be glad, it was needful, because this thy brother was dead, and did live again, he was lost, and was found.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said also unto his disciples, 'A certain man was rich, who had a steward, and he was accused to him as scattering his goods;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having called him, he said to him, What [is] this I hear about thee? render the account of thy stewardship, for thou mayest not any longer be steward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the steward said in himself, What shall I do, because my lord doth take away the stewardship from me? to dig I am not able, to beg I am ashamed: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have known what I shall do, that, when I may be removed from the stewardship, they may receive me to their houses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having called near each one of his lord's debtors, he said to the first, How much dost thou owe to my lord?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, A hundred baths of oil; and he said to him, Take thy bill, and having sat down write fifty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Afterward to another he said, And thou, how much dost thou owe? and he said, A hundred cors of wheat; and he saith to him, Take thy bill, and write eighty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the lord commended the unrighteous steward that he did prudently, because the sons of this age are more prudent than the sons of the light, in respect to their generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, Make to yourselves friends out of the mammon of unrighteousness, that when ye may fail, they may receive you to the age-during tabernacles.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is faithful in the least, [is] also faithful in much; and he who in the least [is] unrighteous, is also unrighteous in much;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if, then, in the unrighteous mammon ye became not faithful -- the true who will entrust to you?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if in the other's ye became not faithful -- your own, who shall give to you?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'No domestic is able to serve two lords, for either the one he will hate, and the other he will love; or one he will hold to, and of the other he will be heedless; ye are not able to serve God and mammon.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And also the Pharisees, being lovers of money, were hearing all these things, and were deriding him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Ye are those declaring yourselves righteous before men, but God doth know your hearts; because that which among men is high, [is] abomination before God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the law and the prophets [are] till John; since then the reign of God is proclaimed good news, and every one doth press into it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it is easier to the heaven and the earth to pass away, than of the law one tittle to fall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Every one who is sending away his wife, and marrying another, doth commit adultery; and every one who is marrying her sent away from a husband doth commit adultery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And -- a certain man was rich, and was clothed in purple and fine linen, making merry sumptuously every day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there was a certain poor man, by name Lazarus, who was laid at his porch, full of sores,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and desiring to be filled from the crumbs that are falling from the table of the rich man; yea, also the dogs, coming, were licking his sores.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it came to pass, that the poor man died, and that he was carried away by the messengers to the bosom of Abraham -- and the rich man also died, and was buried;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the hades having lifted up his eyes, being in torments, he doth see Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having cried, he said, Father Abraham, deal kindly with me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and may cool my tongue, because I am distressed in this flame.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And Abraham said, Child, remember that thou did receive -- thou -- thy good things in thy life, and Lazarus in like manner the evil things, and now he is comforted, and thou art distressed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and besides all these things, between us and you a great chasm is fixed, so that they who are willing to go over from hence unto you are not able, nor do they from thence to us pass through.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he said, I pray thee, then, father, that thou mayest send him to the house of my father,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I have five brothers, so that he may thoroughly testify to them, that they also may not come to this place of torment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Abraham saith to him, They have Moses and the prophets, let them hear them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, No, father Abraham, but if any one from the dead may go unto them, they will reform.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to him, If Moses and the prophets they do not hear, neither if one may rise out of the dead will they be persuaded.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto the disciples, 'It is impossible for the stumbling blocks not to come, but woe [to him] through whom they come;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: it is more profitable to him if a weighty millstone is put round about his neck, and he hath been cast into the sea, than that he may cause one of these little ones to stumble.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed to yourselves, and, if thy brother may sin in regard to thee, rebuke him, and if he may reform, forgive him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if seven times in the day he may sin against thee, and seven times in the day may turn back to thee, saying, I reform; thou shalt forgive him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the apostles said to the Lord, 'Add to us faith;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Lord said, 'If ye had faith as a grain of mustard, ye would have said to this sycamine, Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea, and it would have obeyed you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, who is he of you -- having a servant ploughing or feeding -- who, to him having come in out of the field, will say, Having come near, recline at meat?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but will not [rather] say to him, Prepare what I may sup, and having girded thyself about, minister to me, till I eat and drink, and after these things thou shalt eat and drink?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath he favour to that servant because he did the things directed? I think not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'So also ye, when ye may have done all the things directed you, say -- We are unprofitable servants, because that which we owed to do -- we have done.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his going on to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he entering into a certain village, there met him ten leprous men, who stood afar off,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they lifted up the voice, saying, 'Jesus, master, deal kindly with us;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen [them], he said to them, 'Having gone on, shew yourselves to the priests;' and it came to pass, in their going, they were cleansed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one of them having seen that he was healed did turn back, with a loud voice glorifying God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he fell upon [his] face at his feet, giving thanks to him, and he was a Samaritan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, 'Were not the ten cleansed, and the nine -- where?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There were not found who did turn back to give glory to God, except this alien;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to him, 'Having risen, be going on, thy faith hath saved thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having been questioned by the Pharisees, when the reign of God doth come, he answered them, and said, 'The reign of God doth not come with observation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor shall they say, Lo, here; or lo, there; for lo, the reign of God is within you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto his disciples, 'Days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and ye shall not behold [it];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they shall say to you, Lo, here; or lo, there; ye may not go away, nor follow;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as the lightning that is lightening out of the one [part] under heaven, to the other part under heaven doth shine, so shall be also the Son of Man in his day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and first it behoveth him to suffer many things, and to be rejected by this generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, as it came to pass in the days of Noah, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of Man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were given in marriage, till the day that Noah entered into the ark, and the deluge came, and destroyed all;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in like manner also, as it came to pass in the days of Lot; they were eating, they were drinking, they were buying, they were selling, they were planting, they were building;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on the day Lot went forth from Sodom, He rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'According to these things it shall be, in the day the Son of Man is revealed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in that day, he who shall be on the house top, and his vessels in the house, let him not come down to take them away; and he in the field, in like manner, let him not turn backward;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: remember the wife of Lot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whoever may seek to save his life, shall lose it; and whoever may lose it, shall preserve it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I say to you, In that night, there shall be two men on one couch, the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: two women shall be grinding at the same place together, the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: two men shall be in the field, the one shall be taken, and the other left.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they answering say to him, 'Where, sir?' and he said to them, 'Where the body [is], there will the eagles be gathered together.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake also a simile to them, that it behoveth [us] always to pray, and not to faint,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'A certain judge was in a certain city -- God he is not fearing, and man he is not regarding --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a widow was in that city, and she was coming unto him, saying, Do me justice on my opponent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he would not for a time, but after these things he said in himself, Even if God I do not fear, and man do not regard,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: yet because this widow doth give me trouble, I will do her justice, lest, perpetually coming, she may plague me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said, 'Hear ye what the unrighteous judge saith:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and shall not God execute the justice to His choice ones, who are crying unto Him day and night -- bearing long in regard to them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say to you, that He will execute the justice to them quickly; but the Son of Man having come, shall he find the faith upon the earth?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake also unto certain who have been trusting in themselves that they were righteous, and have been despising the rest, this simile:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Two men went up to the temple to pray, the one a Pharisee, and the other a tax-gatherer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Pharisee having stood by himself, thus prayed: God, I thank Thee that I am not as the rest of men, rapacious, unrighteous, adulterers, or even as this tax-gatherer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all things -- as many as I possess.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the tax-gatherer, having stood afar off, would not even the eyes lift up to the heaven, but was smiting on his breast, saying, God be propitious to me -- the sinner!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say to you, this one went down declared righteous, to his house, rather than that one: for every one who is exalting himself shall be humbled, and he who is humbling himself shall be exalted.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they were bringing near also the babes, that he may touch them, and the disciples having seen did rebuke them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having called them near, said, 'Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not, for of such is the reign of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to you, Whoever may not receive the reign of God as a little child, may not enter into it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain ruler questioned him, saying, 'Good teacher, what having done -- shall I inherit life age-during?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, 'Why me dost thou call good? no one [is] good, except One -- God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the commands thou hast known: Thou mayest not commit adultery, Thou mayest do no murder, Thou mayest not steal, Thou mayest not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'All these I did keep from my youth;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard these things, Jesus said to him, 'Yet one thing to thee is lacking; all things -- as many as thou hast -- sell, and distribute to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven, and come, be following me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he, having heard these things, became very sorrowful, for he was exceeding rich.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having seen him become very sorrowful, said, 'How hardly shall those having riches enter into the reign of God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it is easier for a camel through the eye of a needle to enter, than for a rich man into the reign of God to enter.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those who heard, said, 'And who is able to be saved?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'The things impossible with men are possible with God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter said, 'Lo, we left all, and did follow thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Verily I say to you, that there is not one who left house, or parents, or brothers, or wife, or children, for the sake of the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who may not receive back manifold more in this time, and in the coming age, life age-during.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken the twelve aside, he said unto them, 'Lo, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things shall be completed -- that have been written through the prophets -- to the Son of Man,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he shall be delivered up to the nations, and shall be mocked, and insulted, and spit upon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having scourged they shall put him to death, and on the third day he shall rise again.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they none of these things understood, and this saying was hid from them, and they were not knowing the things said.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his coming nigh to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting beside the way begging,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard a multitude going by, he was inquiring what this may be,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they brought him word that Jesus the Nazarene doth pass by,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he cried out, saying, 'Jesus, Son of David, deal kindly with me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those going before were rebuking him, that he might be silent, but he was much more crying out, 'Son of David, deal kindly with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having stood, commanded him to be brought unto him, and he having come nigh, he questioned him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'What wilt thou I shall do to thee?' and he said, 'Sir, that I may receive sight.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, 'Receive thy sight; thy faith hath saved thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and presently he did receive sight, and was following him, glorifying God; and all the people, having seen, did give praise to God.
