translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having entered, he was passing through Jericho,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a man, by name called Zaccheus, and he was a chief tax-gatherer, and he was rich,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was seeking to see Jesus, who he is, and was not able for the multitude, because in stature he was small,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having run forward before, he went up on a sycamore, that he may see him, because through that [way] he was about to pass by.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as Jesus came up to the place, having looked up, he saw him, and said unto him, 'Zaccheus, having hastened, come down, for to-day in thy house it behoveth me to remain;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having hastened did come down, and did receive him rejoicing;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen [it], they were all murmuring, saying -- 'With a sinful man he went in to lodge!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zaccheus having stood, said unto the Lord, 'Lo, the half of my goods, sir, I give to the poor, and if of any one anything I did take by false accusation, I give back fourfold.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said unto him -- 'To-day salvation did come to this house, inasmuch as he also is a son of Abraham;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while they are hearing these things, having added he spake a simile, because of his being nigh to Jerusalem, and of their thinking that the reign of God is about presently to be made manifest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He said therefore, 'A certain man of birth went on to a far country, to take to himself a kingdom, and to return,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having called ten servants of his own, he gave to them ten pounds, and said unto them, Do business -- till I come;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his citizens were hating him, and did send an embassy after him, saying, We do not wish this one to reign over us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it came to pass, on his coming back, having taken the kingdom, that he commanded these servants to be called to him, to whom he gave the money, that he might know what any one had done in business.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the first came near, saying, Sir, thy pound did gain ten pounds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to him, Well done, good servant, because in a very little thou didst become faithful, be having authority over ten cities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the second came, saying, Sir, thy pound made five pounds;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said also to this one, And thou, become thou over five cities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And another came, saying, Sir, lo, thy pound, that I had lying away in a napkin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I was afraid of thee, because thou art an austere man; thou takest up what thou didst not lay down, and reapest what thou didst not sow.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he saith to him, Out of thy mouth I will judge thee, evil servant: thou knewest that I am an austere man, taking up what I did not lay down, and reaping what I did not sow!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and wherefore didst thou not give my money to the bank, and I, having come, with interest might have received it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And to those standing by he said, Take from him the pound, and give to him having the ten pounds --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (and they said to him, Sir, he hath ten pounds) --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, that to every one having shall be given, and from him not having, also what he hath shall be taken from him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but those my enemies, who did not wish me to reign over them, bring hither and slay before me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having said these things, he went on before, going up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, as he came nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, unto the mount called of the Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having said, Go away to the village over-against, in which, entering into, ye shall find a colt bound, on which no one of men did ever sit, having loosed it, bring [it];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if any one doth question you, Wherefore do ye loose [it]? thus ye shall say to him -- The Lord hath need of it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those sent, having gone away, found according as he said to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and while they are loosing the colt, its owners said unto them, 'Why loose ye the colt?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said, 'The Lord hath need of it;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they brought it unto Jesus, and having cast their garments upon the colt, they did set Jesus upon it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as he is going, they were spreading their garments in the way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as he is coming nigh now, at the descent of the mount of the Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began rejoicing to praise God with a great voice for all the mighty works they had seen,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'blessed [is] he who is coming, a king in the name of the Lord; peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of the Pharisees from the multitude said unto him, 'Teacher, rebuke thy disciples;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said to them, 'I say to you, that, if these shall be silent, the stones will cry out!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he came nigh, having seen the city, he wept over it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- 'If thou didst know, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things for thy peace; but now they were hid from thine eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because days shall come upon thee, and thine enemies shall cast around thee a rampart, and compass thee round, and press thee on every side,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lay thee low, and thy children within thee, and they shall not leave in thee a stone upon a stone, because thou didst not know the time of thy inspection.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having entered into the temple, he began to cast forth those selling in it, and those buying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying to them, 'It hath been written, My house is a house of prayer -- but ye made it a den of robbers.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests and the scribes were seeking to destroy him -- also the chiefs of the people --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were not finding what they shall do, for all the people were hanging on him, hearing him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, on one of those days, as he is teaching the people in the temple, and proclaiming good news, the chief priests and the scribes, with the elders, came upon [him],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and spake unto him, saying, 'Tell us by what authority thou dost these things? or who is he that gave to thee this authority?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said unto them, 'I will question you -- I also -- one thing, and tell me:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the baptism of John, from heaven was it, or from men?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they reasoned with themselves, saying -- 'If we may say, From heaven, he will say, Wherefore, then, did ye not believe him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if we may say, From men, all the people will stone us, for they are having been persuaded John to be a prophet.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they answered, that they knew not whence [it was],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to them, 'Neither do I say to you by what authority I do these things.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he began to speak unto the people this simile: 'A certain man planted a vineyard, and gave it out to husbandmen, and went abroad for a long time,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and at the season he sent unto the husbandmen a servant, that from the fruit of the vineyard they may give to him, but the husbandmen having beat him, did send [him] away empty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he added to send another servant, and they that one also having beaten and dishonoured, did send away empty;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he added to send a third, and this one also, having wounded, they did cast out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the owner of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my son -- the beloved, perhaps having seen this one, they will do reverence;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen him, the husbandmen reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir; come, we may kill him, that the inheritance may become ours;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having cast him outside of the vineyard, they killed [him]; what, then, shall the owner of the vineyard do to them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He will come, and destroy these husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others.' And having heard, they said, 'Let it not be!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he, having looked upon them, said, 'What, then, is this that hath been written: A stone that the builders rejected -- this became head of a corner?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: every one who hath fallen on that stone shall be broken, and on whom it may fall, it will crush him to pieces.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him in that hour, and they feared the people, for they knew that against them he spake this simile.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, having watched [him], they sent forth liers in wait, feigning themselves to be righteous, that they might take hold of his word, to deliver him up to the rule and to the authority of the governor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they questioned him, saying, 'Teacher, we have known that thou dost say and teach rightly, and dost not accept a person, but in truth the way of God dost teach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it lawful to us to give tribute to Caesar or not?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, having perceived their craftiness, said unto them, 'Why me do ye tempt?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: shew me a denary; of whom hath it an image and superscription?' and they answering said, 'Of Caesar:'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Give back, therefore, the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of God to God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were not able to take hold on his saying before the people, and having wondered at his answer, they were silent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of the Sadducees, who are denying that there is a rising again, having come near, questioned him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Teacher, Moses wrote to us, If any one's brother may die, having a wife, and he may die childless -- that his brother may take the wife, and may raise up seed to his brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'There were, then, seven brothers, and the first having taken a wife, died childless,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the second took the wife, and he died childless,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the third took her, and in like manner also the seven -- they left not children, and they died;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and last of all died also the woman:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in the rising again, then, of which of them doth she become wife? -- for the seven had her as wife.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'The sons of this age do marry and are given in marriage,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but those accounted worthy to obtain that age, and the rising again that is out of the dead, neither marry, nor are they given in marriage;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for neither are they able to die any more -- for they are like messengers -- and they are sons of God, being sons of the rising again.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the Bush, since he doth call the Lord, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and He is not a God of dead men, but of living, for all live to Him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of the scribes answering said, 'Teacher, thou didst say well;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and no more durst they question him anything.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'How do they say the Christ to be son of David,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and David himself saith in the Book of Psalms, The Lord said to my lord, Sit thou on my right hand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: till I shall make thine enemies thy footstool;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: David, then, doth call him lord, and how is he his son?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, all the people hearing, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed of the scribes, who are wishing to walk in long robes, and are loving salutations in the markets, and first seats in the synagogues, and first couches in the suppers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who devour the houses of the widows, and for a pretence make long prayers, these shall receive more abundant judgment.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having looked up, he saw those who did cast their gifts to the treasury -- rich men,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saw also a certain poor widow casting there two mites,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'Truly I say to you, that this poor widow did cast in more than all;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all these out of their superabundance did cast into the gifts to God, but this one out of her want, all the living that she had, did cast in.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain saying about the temple, that with goodly stones and devoted things it hath been adorned, he said,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'These things that ye behold -- days will come, in which there shall not be left a stone upon a stone, that shall not be thrown down.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they questioned him, saying, 'Teacher, when, then, shall these things be? and what [is] the sign when these things may be about to happen?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'See -- ye may not be led astray, for many shall come in my name, saying -- I am [he], and the time hath come nigh; go not on then after them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when ye may hear of wars and uprisings, be not terrified, for it behoveth these things to happen first, but the end [is] not immediately.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said he to them, 'Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: great shakings also in every place, and famines, and pestilences, there shall be; fearful things also, and great signs from heaven there shall be;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and before all these, they shall lay on you their hands, and persecute, delivering up to synagogues and prisons, being brought before kings and governors for my name's sake;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it shall become to you for a testimony.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Settle, then, to your hearts, not to meditate beforehand to reply,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I will give to you a mouth and wisdom that all your opposers shall not be able to refute or resist.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye shall be delivered up also by parents, and brothers, and kindred, and friends, and they shall put of you to death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye shall be hated by all because of my name --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a hair out of your head shall not perish;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in your patience possess ye your souls.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when ye may see Jerusalem surrounded by encampments, then know that come nigh did her desolation;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then those in Judea, let them flee to the mountains; and those in her midst, let them depart out; and those in the countries, let them not come in to her;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because these are days of vengeance, to fulfil all things that have been written.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And woe to those with child, and to those giving suck, in those days; for there shall be great distress on the land, and wrath on this people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they shall fall by the mouth of the sword, and shall be led captive to all the nations, and Jerusalem shall be trodden down by nations, till the times of nations be fulfilled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And there shall be signs in sun, and moon, and stars, and on the land [is] distress of nations with perplexity, sea and billow roaring;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: men fainting at heart from fear, and expectation of the things coming on the world, for the powers of the heavens shall be shaken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And then they shall see the Son of Man, coming in a cloud, with power and much glory;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and these things beginning to happen bend yourselves back, and lift up your heads, because your redemption doth draw nigh.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he spake a simile to them: 'See the fig-tree, and all the trees,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: when they may now cast forth, having seen, of yourselves ye know that now is the summer nigh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also ye, when ye may see these things happening, ye know that near is the reign of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to you -- This generation may not pass away till all may have come to pass;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the heaven and the earth shall pass away, but my words may not pass away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And take heed to yourselves, lest your hearts may be weighed down with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and anxieties of life, and suddenly that day may come on you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as a snare it shall come on all those dwelling on the face of all the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: watch ye, then, in every season, praying that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that are about to come to pass, and to stand before the Son of Man.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was during the days in the temple teaching, and during the nights, going forth, he was lodging at the mount called of Olives;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the people were coming early unto him in the temple to hear him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the feast of the unleavened food was coming nigh, that is called Passover,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how they may take him up, for they were afraid of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Adversary entered into Judas, who is surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he, having gone away, spake with the chief priests and the magistrates, how he might deliver him up to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they rejoiced, and covenanted to give him money,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he agreed, and was seeking a favourable season to deliver him up to them without tumult.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day of the unleavened food came, in which it was behoving the passover to be sacrificed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sent Peter and John, saying, 'Having gone on, prepare to us the passover, that we may eat;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said to him, 'Where wilt thou that we might prepare?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'Lo, in your entering into the city, there shall meet you a man, bearing a pitcher of water, follow him to the house where he doth go in,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye shall say to the master of the house, The Teacher saith to thee, Where is the guest-chamber where the passover with my disciples I may eat?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall show you a large upper room furnished, there make ready;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having gone away, found as he hath said to them, and they made ready the passover.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when the hour come, he reclined (at meat), and the twelve apostles with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto them, 'With desire I did desire to eat this passover with you before my suffering,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, that no more may I eat of it till it may be fulfilled in the reign of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken a cup, having given thanks, he said, 'Take this and divide to yourselves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you that I may not drink of the produce of the vine till the reign of God may come.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken bread, having given thanks, he brake and gave to them, saying, 'This is my body, that for you is being given, this do ye -- to remembrance of me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In like manner, also, the cup after the supping, saying, 'This cup [is] the new covenant in my blood, that for you is being poured forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, lo, the hand of him delivering me up [is] with me on the table,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and indeed the Son of Man doth go according to what hath been determined; but woe to that man through whom he is being delivered up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they began to reason among themselves, who then of them it may be, who is about to do this thing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there happened also a strife among them -- who of them is accounted to be greater.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'The kings of the nations do exercise lordship over them, and those exercising authority upon them are called benefactors;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but ye [are] not so, but he who is greater among you -- let him be as the younger; and he who is leading, as he who is ministering;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for who is greater? he who is reclining (at meat), or he who is ministering? is it not he who is reclining (at meat)? and I -- I am in your midst as he who is ministering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye -- ye are those who have remained with me in my temptations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I appoint to you, as my Father did appoint to me, a kingdom,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that ye may eat and may drink at my table, in my kingdom, and may sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord said, 'Simon, Simon, lo, the Adversary did ask you for himself to sift as the wheat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I besought for thee, that thy faith may not fail; and thou, when thou didst turn, strengthen thy brethren.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to him, 'Sir, with thee I am ready both to prison and to death to go;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'I say to thee, Peter, a cock shall not crow to-day, before thrice thou mayest disown knowing me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'When I sent you without bag, and scrip, and sandals, did ye lack anything?' and they said, 'Nothing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said he to them, 'But, now, he who is having a bag, let him take [it] up, and in like manner also a scrip; and he who is not having, let him sell his garment, and buy a sword,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, that yet this that hath been written it behoveth to be fulfilled in me: And with lawless ones he was reckoned, for also the things concerning me have an end.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said, 'Sir, lo, here [are] two swords;' and he said to them, 'It is sufficient.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone forth, he went on, according to custom, to the mount of the Olives, and his disciples also followed him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come to the place, he said to them, 'Pray ye not to enter into temptation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he was withdrawn from them, as it were a stone's cast, and having fallen on the knees he was praying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Father, if Thou be counselling to make this cup pass from me -- ;but, not my will, but Thine be done.' --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there appeared to him a messenger from heaven strengthening him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been in agony, he was more earnestly praying, and his sweat became, as it were, great drops of blood falling upon the ground.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having risen up from the prayer, having come unto the disciples, he found them sleeping from the sorrow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'Why do ye sleep? having risen, pray that ye may not enter into temptation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while he is speaking, lo, a multitude, and he who is called Judas, one of the twelve, was coming before them, and he came nigh to Jesus to kiss him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'Judas, with a kiss the Son of Man dost thou deliver up?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those about him, having seen what was about to be, said to him, 'Sir, shall we smite with a sword?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a certain one of them smote the servant of the chief priest, and took off his right ear,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus answering said, 'Suffer ye thus far,' and having touched his ear, he healed him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to those having come upon him -- chief priests, and magistrates of the temple, and elders -- 'As upon a robber have ye come forth, with swords and sticks?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: while daily I was with you in the temple, ye did stretch forth no hands against me; but this is your hour and the power of the darkness.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken him, they led and brought him to the house of the chief priest. And Peter was following afar off,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having kindled a fire in the midst of the court, and having sat down together, Peter was sitting in the midst of them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain maid having seen him sitting at the light, and having earnestly looked at him, she said, 'And this one was with him!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he disowned him, saying, 'Woman, I have not known him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after a little, another having seen him, said, 'And thou art of them!' and Peter said, 'Man, I am not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And one hour, as it were, having intervened, a certain other was confidently affirming, saying, 'Of a truth this one also was with him, for he is also a Galilean;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Peter said, 'Man, I have not known what thou sayest;' and presently, while he is speaking, a cock crew.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord having turned did look on Peter, and Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he said to him -- 'Before a cock shall crow, thou mayest disown me thrice;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Peter having gone without, wept bitterly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the men who were holding Jesus were mocking him, beating [him];

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having blindfolded him, they were striking him on the face, and were questioning him, saying, 'Prophesy who he is who smote thee?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and many other things, speaking evilly, they spake in regard to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when it became day there was gathered together the eldership of the people, chief priests also, and scribes, and they led him up to their own sanhedrim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'If thou be the Christ, tell us.' And he said to them, 'If I may tell you, ye will not believe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if I also question [you], ye will not answer me or send me away;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: henceforth, there shall be the Son of Man sitting on the right hand of the power of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they all said, 'Thou, then, art the Son of God?' and he said unto them, 'Ye say [it], because I am;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said, 'What need yet have we of testimony? for we ourselves did hear [it] from his mouth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having risen, the whole multitude of them did lead him to Pilate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and began to accuse him, saying, 'This one we found perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, saying himself to be Christ a king.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate questioned him, saying, 'Thou art the king of the Jews?' and he answering him, said, 'Thou dost say [it].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate said unto the chief priests, and the multitude, 'I find no fault in this man;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were the more urgent, saying -- 'He doth stir up the people, teaching throughout the whole of Judea -- having begun from Galilee -- unto this place.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate having heard of Galilee, questioned if the man is a Galilean,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having known that he is from the jurisdiction of Herod, he sent him back unto Herod, he being also in Jerusalem in those days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Herod having seen Jesus did rejoice exceedingly, for he was wishing for a long [time] to see him, because of hearing many things about him, and he was hoping some sign to see done by him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and was questioning him in many words, and he answered him nothing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests and the scribes stood vehemently accusing him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Herod with his soldiers having set him at nought, and having mocked, having put around him gorgeous apparel, did send him back to Pilate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and both Pilate and Herod became friends on that day with one another, for they were before at enmity between themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate having called together the chief priests, and the rulers, and the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: said unto them, 'Ye brought to me this man as perverting the people, and lo, I before you having examined, found in this man no fault in those things ye bring forward against him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: no, nor yet Herod, for I sent you back unto him, and lo, nothing worthy of death is having been done by him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having chastised, therefore, I will release him,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for it was necessary for him to release to them one at every feast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they cried out -- the whole multitude -- saying, 'Away with this one, and release to us Barabbas,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who had been, because of a certain sedition made in the city, and murder, cast into prison.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pilate again then -- wishing to release Jesus -- called to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but they were calling out, saying, 'Crucify, crucify him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he a third time said unto them, 'Why, what evil did he? no cause of death did I find in him; having chastised him, then, I will release [him].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they were pressing with loud voices asking him to be crucified, and their voices, and those of the chief priests, were prevailing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Pilate gave judgment for their request being done,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he released him who because of sedition and murder hath been cast into the prison, whom they were asking, and Jesus he gave up to their will.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as they led him away, having taken hold on Simon, a certain Cyrenian, coming from the field, they put on him the cross, to bear [it] behind Jesus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there was following him a great multitude of the people, and of women, who also were beating themselves and lamenting him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having turned unto them, said, 'Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but for yourselves weep ye, and for your children;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, lo, days do come, in which they shall say, Happy the barren, and wombs that did not bare, and paps that did not give suck;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then they shall begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us, and to the hills, Cover us; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, if in the green tree they do these things -- in the dry what may happen?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were also others -- two evil-doers -- with him, to be put to death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when they came to the place that is called Skull, there they crucified him and the evil-doers, one on the right hand and one on the left.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said, 'Father, forgive them, for they have not known what they do;' and parting his garments they cast a lot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the people were standing, looking on, and the rulers also were sneering with them, saying, 'Others he saved, let him save himself, if this be the Christ, the choice one of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And mocking him also were the soldiers, coming near and offering vinegar to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there was also a superscription written over him, in letters of Greek, and Roman, and Hebrew, 'This is the King of the Jews.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And one of the evil-doers who were hanged, was speaking evil of him, saying, 'If thou be the Christ, save thyself and us.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the other answering, was rebuking him, saying, 'Dost thou not even fear God, that thou art in the same judgment?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we indeed righteously, for things worthy of what we did we receive back, but this one did nothing out of place;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to Jesus, 'Remember me, lord, when thou mayest come in thy reign;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'Verily I say to thee, To-day with me thou shalt be in the paradise.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it was, as it were, the sixth hour, and darkness came over all the land till the ninth hour,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sun was darkened, and the vail of the sanctuary was rent in the midst,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having cried with a loud voice, Jesus said, 'Father, to Thy hands I commit my spirit;' and these things having said, he breathed forth the spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the centurion having seen what was done, did glorify God, saying, 'Really this man was righteous;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the multitudes who were come together to this sight, beholding the things that came to pass, smiting their breasts did turn back;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all his acquaintances stood afar off, and women who did follow him from Galilee, beholding these things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, a man, by name Joseph, being a counsellor, a man good and righteous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: -- he was not consenting to their counsel and deed -- from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who also himself was expecting the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he, having gone near to Pilate, asked the body of Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken it down, he wrapped it in fine linen, and placed it in a tomb hewn out, where no one was yet laid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day was a preparation, and sabbath was approaching,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the women also who have come with him out of Galilee having followed after, beheld the tomb, and how his body was placed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having turned back, they made ready spices and ointments, and on the sabbath, indeed, they rested, according to the command.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the first of the sabbaths, at early dawn, they came to the tomb, bearing the spices they made ready, and certain [others] with them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they found the stone having been rolled away from the tomb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone in, they found not the body of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, while they are perplexed about this, that lo, two men stood by them in glittering apparel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and on their having become afraid, and having inclined the face to the earth, they said to them, 'Why do ye seek the living with the dead?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he is not here, but was raised; remember how he spake to you, being yet in Galilee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- It behoveth the Son of Man to be delivered up to the hands of sinful men, and to be crucified, and the third day to rise again.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they remembered his sayings,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having turned back from the tomb told all these things to the eleven, and to all the rest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it was the Magdalene Mary, and Joanna, and Mary of James, and the other women with them, who told unto the apostles these things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their sayings appeared before them as idle talk, and they were not believing them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter having risen, did run to the tomb, and having stooped down he seeth the linen clothes lying alone, and he went away to his own home, wondering at that which was come to pass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, lo, two of them were going on during that day to a village, distant sixty furlongs from Jerusalem, the name of which [is] Emmaus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were conversing with one another about all these things that have happened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass in their conversing and reasoning together, that Jesus himself, having come nigh, was going on with them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their eyes were holden so as not to know him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said unto them, 'What [are] these words that ye exchange with one another, walking, and ye are sad?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the one, whose name was Cleopas, answering, said unto him, 'Art thou alone such a stranger in Jerusalem, that thou hast not known the things that came to pass in it in these days?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'What things?' And they said to him, 'The things about Jesus of Nazareth, who became a man -- a prophet -- powerful in deed and word, before God and all the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how also the chief priests and our rulers did deliver him up to a judgment of death, and crucified him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and we were hoping that he it is who is about to redeem Israel, and also with all these things, this third day is passing to-day, since these things happened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And certain women of ours also astonished us, coming early to the tomb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and not having found his body, they came, saying also to have seen an apparition of messengers, who say he is alive,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and certain of those with us went away unto the tomb, and found as even the women said, and him they saw not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said unto them, 'O inconsiderate and slow in heart, to believe on all that the prophets spake!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Was it not behoving the Christ these things to suffer, and to enter into his glory?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having begun from Moses, and from all the prophets, he was expounding to them in all the Writings the things about himself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they came nigh to the village whither they were going, and he made an appearance of going on further,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they constrained him, saying, 'Remain with us, for it is toward evening,' and the day did decline, and he went in to remain with them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, in his reclining (at meat) with them, having taken the bread, he blessed, and having broken, he was giving to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, and he became unseen by them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said one to another, 'Was not our heart burning within us, as he was speaking to us in the way, and as he was opening up to us the Writings?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they, having risen up the same hour, turned back to Jerusalem, and found gathered together the eleven, and those with them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying -- 'The Lord was raised indeed, and was seen by Simon;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were telling the things in the way, and how he was made known to them in the breaking of the bread,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as they are speaking these things, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith to them, 'Peace -- to you;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and being amazed, and becoming affrighted, they were thinking themselves to see a spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'Why are ye troubled? and wherefore do reasonings come up in your hearts?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: see my hands and my feet, that I am he; handle me and see, because a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me having.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having said this, he shewed to them the hands and the feet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and while they are not believing from the joy, and wondering, he said to them, 'Have ye anything here to eat?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they gave to him part of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken, he did eat before them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them, 'These [are] the words that I spake unto you, being yet with you, that it behoveth to be fulfilled all the things that are written in the Law of Moses, and the Prophets, and the Psalms, about me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then opened he up their understanding to understand the Writings,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to them -- 'Thus it hath been written, and thus it was behoving the Christ to suffer, and to rise out of the dead the third day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and reformation and remission of sins to be proclaimed in his name to all the nations, beginning from Jerusalem:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye -- ye are witnesses of these things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, lo, I do send the promise of my Father upon you, but ye -- abide ye in the city of Jerusalem till ye be clothed with power from on high.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he led them forth without -- unto Bethany, and having lifted up his hands he did bless them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it came to pass, in his blessing them, he was parted from them, and was borne up to the heaven;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having bowed before him, did turn back to Jerusalem with great joy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.
